sound_type: ram clash -- resonant cranial impact CRACK. Broadband 100-5,000+ Hz, peak 500-2,000 Hz. Up to 800 lbs force. Audible 1-2+ miles. E=6
ecosystem: Rocky slopes, alpine meadows, canyon walls. Fall rut (October-December). Rocky Mountain and Cascade populations.
family: Bovidae (third entry -- Mountain Goat at 203, kid at 204)

coordinates.description: Bighorn Sheep in Cascades and eastern Washington rocky slopes. Ram combat during fall rut.

taxonomy_note.description: Third Bovidae entry. First impact-generated sound. Ram clash at E=6.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Bovidae: Mountain Goat Bleat (203, E=4), Mountain Goat Kid Mew (204, E=3), Bighorn Sheep Clash (205, E=6).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Ovis from Latin 'sheep'. canadensis for Canada/North America.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Ovis -- sheep genus. Bighorn Sheep is the iconic North American wild sheep.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Artiodactyla. Third Bovidae entry. Bovidae energy arc: 4-3-6.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Bovidae: third entry. Impact sound. Maximum family energy.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Rocky slope specialist. Skull adapted for repeated high-force impact.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Broadband 100-5,000+ Hz. Not a vocalization but an impact-generated sound.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Clash: broadband. Grunt: 50-200 Hz. Snort: brief, nasal.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Impact: <0.1 sec. Echo/decay: 1-3 sec in canyon. Contest: minutes to hours.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single sharp crack per impact. Repeated as charge-clash-separate-pause cycle.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Charge, CRACK, separate, pause. Repeated dozens of times per contest.
song_metrics.timbre: Sharp, resonant crack. Keratin horn sheaths vibrate on impact. Not a thud -- a CRACK.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=6. Maximum Bovidae. Impact-generated. 1-2+ mile carry. Canyon-amplified.
song_metrics.tempo: Charge cycle: variable. Minutes between clashes. Hours per contest.
song_metrics.context: Fall rut. Ram-on-ram combat. Mating dominance. Rocky slopes and canyons.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Subspecies vary in size (Rocky Mountain > Desert). Clash force proportional to body mass.
song_metrics.key_property: RAM CLASH. Resonant CRACK. 800 lbs. E=6. FIRST IMPACT SOUND. Third Bovidae. Bovidae arc 4-3-6. Canyon-amplified. v1.49.346.

pelage.overall: Brown to gray-brown coat. White rump patch. Massive curled horns (rams). 125-300 lbs (rams).
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Large curled horns on rams. White rump patch. Muscular build. Rocky terrain.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Thicker winter coat. Summer: shorter, lighter brown.

physical_description.overall: Rams: 125-300 lbs. Massive curved horns up to 30+ lbs combined. Double-layered skull. Ewes: smaller, shorter horns.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Large brown sheep with massive curled horns on rocky terrain. White rump patch. Muscular. If you hear a crack in the canyon during fall, look for rams.
physical_description.field_id: Large sheep on rocky slopes. Rams have distinctive curled horns visible at distance. Fall rut: look for two rams charging. The crack carries far.

nodes.title: First Impact-Generated Sound
nodes.description: Not a vocalization but a collision. 800 lbs of force converted to resonant CRACK. Opens new acoustic category for the engine.

nodes.title: Skull Engineering -- Biological Crumple Zone
nodes.description: Double-layered skull. Cancellous bone struts. Frontal sinuses as pneumatic cushions. Cervical reinforcement. Impact-absorption chain.

nodes.title: Horn Curl Measurement
nodes.description: 3/4 curl to full curl (360 degrees). 30+ lbs combined. 7-8 years to full curl. The curl directs force into bone-keratin base.

nodes.title: Bovidae Energy Arc -- 4-3-6
nodes.description: Mountain Goat bleat (E=4), kid mew (E=3), Bighorn Sheep clash (E=6). Quiet contact, quieter infant, MAXIMUM impact. Dramatic contrast.

cross_references.relationship: BOVIDAE 1/3. Adult bleat (E=4). Contact call. The family baseline before impact.
BOVIDAE 2/3. Kid mew (E=3). Thin infant voice. Bovidae arc: 4-3-6.
Warning vs. combat. Bear huffs to avoid. Ram clashes to win. Different strategies in the E=5-6 range.
ENERGY MATCH (E=6 + E=6 = 12). Impact force: 800 lbs cranial (ram) vs. power trio volume (band). Both maximum.
